title: A Wretched Hive
summary: The heroes travel to Takodana to infiltrate Varga Besadii Drokko's fortress, posing as entertainment to steal the encryption keys to Varth's fortune. Over several days in the Hutt's court, they navigate gladiatorial spectacle, slave traders, an ISB double agent, and a nighttime assassination attempt — only to discover a captive Jedi Master in the dungeons. The escape costs lives: Demos dies fighting, Varga flees to Bespin, and Varth executes the man the heroes just rescued.

gearNotes.reason: Varth coaches the heroes over encrypted comms from outside Varga's palace. Open comlinks in a Hutt fortress means blown cover. Hard permission gate for the entire court sequence.
The entire mission objective is slicing Varga's datapad to extract the encryption keys. Without a proper slicing interface, the core technical challenge is stepped down or impossible.
High-security detention block locks where Jedi Master Denia is held. Without one, bypassing these suffers heavy penalties.
The Tech's audition — ordered to fix Varga's hover-sled with a blaster at their head. No toolkit means stepped-down rolls on a pass/fail moment.
Multi-scene combat from the hit squad through the fortress escape. No medical facilities available.
Underworld social currency in Blackwind Point. Opens doors in criminal circles where credits draw suspicion. Varth's shopping list should include this.
Plant bugs near the Iron Ring delegation or Lady Sinde's private meetings. The intel from these events reveals Project Leviathan's slave labor connection.
Intercepting unencrypted palace communications — security patrol schedules, delivery timings, who talks to whom.
Recording evidence of slave trade and Imperial deals. Leverage material for later adventures.
Heavy combat droid at the filtration plant and elite security droids in the dungeon. Ion damage is the purpose-built counter.
Sealed blast doors during fortress lockdown. Overload Mode cuts through any door as a single action.
Covering movement through hostile corridors during lockdown and the hangar escape.
Quick terminal bypass in the dungeon to access stasis controls. Consumable, single action.
Hangar bay area denial against clustered Stormtroopers.
Multi-scene fighting with no rest. Someone is running on fumes by the hangar escape.

scenes.title: Blackwind Point
scenes.subtitle: The Landing Tax
scenes.readAloud:
You disembark at Blackwind Point — a muddy, desperate shantytown on the far shore of Nymeve Lake. The air is thick with the smell of rotting vegetation and desperation. Refugees huddle in makeshift shelters while criminals eye your gear with open hunger.

The growl of repulsor-bikes cuts through the noise. A swoop gang circles your landing pad, engines revving. Their leader — a scarred human woman with a vibro-blade strapped to her leg — pulls up and kills her engine.

"Welcome to the other side of the lake. Landing tax is five hundred credits. Non-negotiable."
scenes.gmNotes:
This scene is a standalone social encounter with a combat fallback. Kessra and the Vipers demand 500 credits. The crew either pays, talks their way out, or fights.

KESSRA CONSEQUENCE GATE: This is the most important mechanical note in the scene. How the crew handles Kessra determines a conditional event inside Varga's fortress later. If they humiliate her (fight and win decisively, or intimidate her publicly), Kessra takes it personally. She reappears inside Varga's fortress during the court phase (Part 2, Scene 3) as a hired security contractor and springs an aviary ambush targeting specific heroes by name. If they pay or handle it diplomatically, Kessra does NOT reappear — no grudge, no aviary scene.

PAYING: If the crew pays the 500cr, Kessra takes it and waves them through. The Vipers watch but don't hassle them further. The crew is now marked as easy targets — pickpockets and grifters will approach them in the encampment. Word spreads that the newcomers fold under pressure.

FIGHTING: If it turns violent, Kessra fights from her swoop. The Vipers fan out and use hit-and-run tactics. The mud is difficult terrain for the crew but the swoops ignore it. If Kessra is beaten, the gang scatters. Local respect earned — but Kessra remembers.

AFTER THE ENCOUNTER: Regardless of outcome, the crew is now standing in Blackwind Point with a job to do — find Warrick Raden. Varth has briefed them: a Devaronian fixer who can get them into Varga's court. All they have is a name. The encampment stretches out before them — refugees, junk traders, cantinas, spice dealers. Time to start asking questions.

npcs.name: Kessra
npcs.notes: Scarred human woman. Leader of The Vipers. Consequence gate — may reappear in fortress if humiliated here.
npcs.behaviour: Demands 500 credits landing tax. Tests the heroes' nerve. If humiliated, holds a personal grudge that triggers the aviary ambush in Part 2.
dialogue: "Landing tax is five hundred credits. Non-negotiable."
"You think you're tough? Everyone who comes here thinks they're tough. Most of them are wrong."
"I remember faces. Especially the ones that embarrass me."

maneuver.name: Reposition
maneuver.description: Modifies Move. Move up to 2 zones. If the Harrier ends farther from all enemies than it started, +1 Power on next attack.

gambit.name: Pinning Fire
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Slowed] Tactical.

exploit.name: Snap Shot
exploit.trigger: A PC uses Move to enter or cross a zone within weapon range.
exploit.description: Immediately attack that PC at -2 Power.

threatBuild.notes: Kessra is a recurring NPC. Consequence gate: humiliation here triggers aviary ambush in Part 2.

npcs.name: The Vipers
npcs.notes: Armed with blasters and vibro-blades. Intimidation over combat skill.

npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Exposed] + free Move 1 zone (no Opening)

maneuver.name: Slip Away
maneuver.description: Modifies Move. When disengaging, does not present an Opening to the target of its last attack. Move up to 2 zones.

gambit.name: Hamstring
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Slowed] Tactical.

exploit.name: Pursuit
exploit.trigger: A PC in the Harrier's zone uses Move to leave.
exploit.description: Harrier immediately moves to the same zone (no Opening).

threatBuild.notes: Minion gang. Masterful+ = takedown. Paper tigers — flee if Kessra falls.

scenes.hazards: Muddy terrain at the landing pad — difficult terrain, movement costs double. Swoops ignore this. Ferrocrete pad edges provide stable footing.

decisions.choice: Pay Kessra's landing tax
decisions.consequence: Marked as easy targets. Pickpockets and grifters will target you in the encampment. No aviary ambush later.

decisions.choice: Humiliate Kessra (fight or intimidate)
decisions.consequence: Local respect earned. But Kessra holds a grudge — she reappears inside the fortress for the aviary ambush (Part 2, Scene 3).

narrativeLinks.note: Kessra's humiliation here triggers the aviary ambush in Part 2 Scene 3.

encounters.name: Viper Shakedown
encounters.description: Kessra and 6 Vipers demand 500 credits. Social challenge with combat fallback. If fighting starts, Kessra fights from her swoop while Vipers fan out for hit-and-run.
encounters.tactics: Kessra leads verbally — she wants money, not blood. Vipers fan out to cut off exits. If a hero draws a weapon, that's leverage for an Intimidation check, not a trigger for combat. If fighting starts, swoops use hit-and-run in the mud. If Kessra falls, the Vipers scatter.

environmentMechanics.name: Muddy Landing Pad
environmentMechanics.effect: Difficult terrain. Movement costs double. Swoops ignore this.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay on the ferrocrete pad edges for stable footing.

rewards.narrative: Kessra encounter resolved. The crew is free to move through Blackwind Point.

pacing.openingBeat: The Vipers circle the landing pad. Engines cut. Kessra makes her demand.
pacing.risingAction: Negotiation, intimidation, or blasters drawn. The crew makes their choice.
pacing.climax: The moment the crew pays, talks down, or fights Kessra. One confrontation, one decision.
pacing.resolution: The Vipers leave or scatter. Blackwind Point stretches out before the crew. Time to find Raden.

disciplineChallenges.context: Staring down Kessra and the Vipers to avoid paying landing tax.

control.failure: Kessra doesn't flinch. The Vipers close in. Pay the tax or fight.
control.mastery: Kessra backs off AND warns the Vipers to stay clear. Word spreads — the new arrivals are dangerous. +1 tier on the next social check in the encampment.

effect.fleeting: Kessra hesitates, then sneers and waves you past. The tax is dropped but the Vipers watch you closely.
effect.masterful: Kessra reads you as genuinely dangerous. Backs off with visible unease. Locals who saw it start treating you with wary respect.
effect.legendary: Kessra physically recoils. She orders the Vipers to give you a wide berth. For the rest of the encampment stay, no one hassles you. Free passage everywhere.

disciplineChallenges.context: Talking Kessra down with diplomacy or flattery — acknowledging her authority while redirecting.

control.failure: Kessra isn't charmed. She's heard every line. Pay or deal with the consequences.
control.mastery: Kessra is genuinely amused. She waves the tax AND offers a piece of local intel: 'You want to survive here? Avoid the east market after dark.'

effect.fleeting: Kessra pauses. She's not convinced but she's entertained. Tax reduced to 200 credits. She lets you pass with a smirk.
effect.masterful: Kessra laughs — genuinely. She likes you. Tax waived. 'First one's free. Next time you pay double.' No grudge, no consequence.
effect.legendary: Kessra is disarmed. She not only drops the tax but tells a Viper to walk you to the cantina. 'Buy these ones a drink. They've got guts.' Local contacts established.

scenes.title: Working the Encampment
scenes.subtitle: Finding Raden
scenes.readAloud:
Blackwind Point is a sprawl of desperation and opportunism. Muddy lanes wind between scrap-metal shelters and sagging tarpaulin stalls. A Rodian hawking stolen power cells argues with a Gran over a crate of expired ration packs. Somewhere a child is crying. A pair of Aqualish dockhands sit on overturned fuel drums, watching you with flat, hostile eyes.

Somewhere in this place — or beyond it — a Devaronian fixer named Warrick Raden is hiding. Varth said he could get you inside Varga's court. All you have is a name and a description. Start asking questions.
scenes.gmNotes:
GROUP CHALLENGE: This scene uses a Victory Point (VP) accumulation mechanic. The crew fans out across Blackwind Point and works different angles to track down Raden's location. Each PC picks a discipline and describes their approach.

MECHANICS:
- Tier 1 / Power 3 across all disciplines
- Each PC rolls Control (stepped down 1 die for T1). Control 4+ = success.
- On success, roll Power vs P3 to determine effect tier:
  • Fleeting (Power loses or ties) = 1 VP
  • Masterful (Power wins) = 2 VP
  • Legendary (Power wins decisively) = 3 VP
  • Mastery (Control 8+) = +1 VP added to the tier result
- 10 VP total = they've pinpointed Raden's location
- Below 10, they can attempt more rolls or go looking with incomplete info

FAILURE CONSEQUENCE: A failed Control roll (below 4) means someone noticed the crew asking around. Word travels through the encampment. Each failure increases the risk that Varga's people — or the Trandoshan hit squad already hunting Raden — learn that someone else is looking for the same man. The GM should narrate the growing tension: a merchant who suddenly clams up, a figure watching from a doorway, whispered conversations that stop when the crew approaches.

VP THRESHOLDS — Read aloud or paraphrase at each threshold:

3 VP: 'A Devaronian? Yeah, I saw one drinking at the Drowned Sarlacc three, four days ago. Kept to himself. Haven't seen him since.' — A sighting, but cold. He was here.

5 VP: 'That Devaronian — he was talking to old Mekka the parts dealer. Bought a bunch of ration packs and a portable charge cell. Like someone planning to disappear.' — He's hiding somewhere, stocking up to stay gone.

7 VP: 'Two days ago I saw a Devaronian running through the east market. Some of Varga's boys were on his tail. He cut toward the swamp and they pulled up at the tree line. Didn't follow him in.' — Direction established. He went into the swamp.

9 VP: 'There's an old water filtration plant out past the swamp edge. Imperial-era, been abandoned for years. Saw smoke from that direction yesterday morning. Nobody goes out there.' — Specific location narrowed down.

10 VP: 'That's where your Devaronian is. The filtration plant. I hauled water purifiers out there two years back — there's a maintenance shed on the west side with a working lock. If he's hiding anywhere, it's that shed.' — Confirmed. Go.

ENCOURAGING DIFFERENT APPROACHES: Each PC should describe their approach narratively. The discipline they choose should match what they're doing. The GM provides the VP threshold readout based on total accumulated VP, not individual rolls. Let the players feel the trail building — each piece of information makes the next question more specific.

SCENE ENDS: When the crew hits 10 VP or when they decide they've heard enough and want to go looking with what they have. If they leave before 10 VP, they'll need to search the swamp edge — add a complication to Scene 3 (Raden is harder to find, they stumble into his trip-wires, etc.).

ENCOUNTER NPCs: The encampment should feel alive. Use these locals as color and information sources. Not every NPC has intel — some are just texture. The crew should feel like they're working a real place, not just rolling dice.

npcs.name: Mekka
npcs.notes: Runs a stall near the east market. Sold Raden ration packs and a portable charge cell three days ago. Knows Raden was stocking up to hide.
npcs.behaviour: Shrewd and unhelpful unless given a reason to talk. Credits loosen his tongue. Responds well to Charm. Clams up under Intimidation — he's been shaken down too many times.
dialogue: "A Devaronian? I see a lot of Devaronians. What's in it for me?"
"He bought survival supplies. Ration packs, charge cell, water tabs. Paid in cash — Imperial credits, not Hutt scrip. That's unusual around here."
"I don't know where he went. I sell parts, not directions."

npcs.name: Drelk
npcs.notes: Works the landing pads. Saw Raden fleeing Varga's enforcers two days ago. Watched him cut toward the swamp. Doesn't want trouble.
npcs.behaviour: Sullen and cautious. Will talk if approached without hostility. Responds to shared drinks or a show of solidarity. Won't talk to anyone who looks Imperial.
dialogue: "I mind my own business. You should try it."
"Two days ago. East market. Your Devaronian was running hard. Varga's boys were on him. He went for the swamp — they stopped at the tree line. Smart of them. Stupid of him."
"The swamp takes people. If he went in there, he's either dead or more desperate than I thought."

npcs.name: Nola Shar
npcs.notes: A mother with two children. Arrived from Felucia three weeks ago. She saw smoke from the filtration plant yesterday morning.
npcs.behaviour: Exhausted and wary. Won't volunteer information unless she feels safe. Responds to kindness — a ration pack, a kind word, asking about her children. She doesn't know who Raden is specifically, but she knows the filtration plant.
dialogue: "Please — we don't want trouble. We just need to get to the next transport."
"The filtration plant? It's been abandoned since the Republic fell. But I saw smoke from that direction yesterday when I was washing clothes at the creek. Someone's out there."
"Be careful near the swamp. There are things in that water."

npcs.name: Gorr the Barkeep
npcs.notes: Runs The Drowned Sarlacc — the only cantina in Blackwind Point. Raden drank here four days ago. Gorr doesn't care about Raden but he cares about his bar.
npcs.behaviour: Four arms, three of which are always busy. Hospitable to paying customers. Has no loyalty to anyone but himself. Will trade information for credits or a favor.
dialogue: "Devaronian? Red skin, horns, looked like he hadn't slept in a week? Sat in the back corner four days ago. Nursed one drink for three hours. Then left."
"He was asking about the swamp — what's out there, who goes there. I told him the same thing I tell everyone: nothing good."
"You want another round or are you going to keep asking questions I'm not getting paid for?"

npcs.name: Greez Thakka
npcs.notes: Hauls water purification equipment to settlements around the lake. Knows the filtration plant intimately — he serviced it two years ago. Can describe the maintenance shed.
npcs.behaviour: Gentle and cooperative. Speaks through a translator unit. Happy to help if asked politely. Doesn't understand why anyone would hide at the filtration plant.
dialogue: "The filtration plant? Yes, I know it well. I serviced the purification units there two years ago, before the last of the operators left."
"There is a maintenance shed on the west side. It has a working lock — Imperial standard, five-digit code. Whoever went in would need to slice it or have the code."
"It is not a comfortable place. But it is hidden. The swamp growth has nearly swallowed it."

scenes.hazards: The encampment itself is not physically dangerous, but drawing too much attention is. Failed investigation attempts alert Varga's network or the Trandoshan hit squad already hunting Raden.

decisions.choice: Pursue the investigation thoroughly (reach 10 VP)
decisions.consequence: Complete picture — exact location of Raden's hiding spot. Clean approach to the filtration plant.

decisions.choice: Leave early with partial information
decisions.consequence: Must search the swamp edge. Raden's trip-wires catch the crew off guard. Complications at the filtration plant.

narrativeLinks.note: VP total determines how prepared the crew is when they arrive at the filtration plant.

groupChallenge.name: Tracking Raden
groupChallenge.description: The crew fans out across Blackwind Point to track down Warrick Raden's location.
groupChallenge.failureConsequence: Someone noticed you asking about Raden. Word travels through the encampment — increased risk of alerting Varga's network or the Trandoshan hit squad.

thresholds.intel: A cold sighting — Raden was drinking at The Drowned Sarlacc four days ago. Kept to himself. Haven't seen him since.
He bought survival supplies from Mekka the parts dealer — ration packs, charge cell, water tabs. Someone planning to disappear.
Two days ago he was seen running through the east market. Varga's enforcers were chasing him. He cut toward the swamp — they stopped at the tree line.
An old water filtration plant at the swamp's edge. Imperial-era, abandoned for years. Smoke was seen from that direction yesterday morning.
Confirmed — the filtration plant. Maintenance shed on the west side with a working lock. That's where he's hiding.

eligibleDisciplines.approach: Methodical questioning, cross-referencing stories, following physical trail signs through the market.
Buying someone a drink, earning trust from a refugee or trader who saw something.
Posing as someone with business for Raden, getting criminals to talk without realizing they're being pumped.
Reading body language in the crowd, spotting who gets nervous when the Devaronian is mentioned.
Leaning on a local who clearly knows something but won't talk.
Working the underworld channels, trading favors for information, speaking the right language.

environmentMechanics.name: Encampment Noise
environmentMechanics.effect: Word spreads that outsiders are asking about a Devaronian. Varga's informants may pick up the trail. The Trandoshan hit squad may accelerate their timeline.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Succeed on checks. Use disciplines that draw less attention (Insight, Investigation) over loud approaches (Intimidation).

rewards.narrative: The crew has pieced together Raden's trail. The filtration plant at the swamp's edge awaits.

pacing.openingBeat: The crew stands in Blackwind Point with nothing but a name. The encampment stretches out — chaotic, hostile, alive.
pacing.risingAction: Each PC works a different angle. Breadcrumbs accumulate — a sighting, supplies purchased, a chase toward the swamp. The trail builds.
pacing.climax: The final piece falls into place. The filtration plant. The maintenance shed. They know where Raden is.
pacing.resolution: The crew regroups and heads for the swamp's edge. The trail is fresh — but they're not the only ones following it.

disciplineChallenges.context: Methodical questioning of encampment locals. Cross-referencing stories. Following physical trail signs.

control.failure: Conflicting stories. No clear lead — and the person you asked is now watching you from across the market.
control.mastery: Strong lead + mastery bonus VP. The source volunteers additional context unprompted.

effect.fleeting: A single data point — a sighting, a direction, a timeframe. One piece of the puzzle.
effect.masterful: A clear, corroborated lead. Two pieces click together — the picture sharpens.
effect.legendary: A breakthrough. The source connects dots the crew hadn't considered. Major progress on the trail.

disciplineChallenges.context: Buying a drink, earning trust, making a refugee or trader feel safe enough to talk.

control.failure: The local clams up. They've been burned before by strangers asking questions.
control.mastery: The source opens up completely and offers to introduce you to someone who knows more.

effect.fleeting: A hesitant answer. They saw something but they're not sure about the details.
effect.masterful: A willing informant. They tell you what they know and wish you luck.
effect.legendary: A genuine connection. They not only tell you everything but offer to help — a guide to the swamp edge, a warning about dangers.

disciplineChallenges.context: Posing as someone with business for the Devaronian. Getting criminals to talk without realizing they're being pumped for information.

control.failure: Your cover story doesn't hold. The local suspects you're not who you claim — and now they're curious about why you're really asking.
control.mastery: The mark doesn't just buy it — they actively try to help you find Raden, thinking there's money in it for them.

effect.fleeting: A vague direction. 'Try the east side' — not much, but something.
effect.masterful: Detailed intel wrapped in a lie. They think they're helping a business partner and give you real information.
effect.legendary: They escort you partway, filling in details the whole time. By the time you split off, you've got a near-complete picture.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading body language in the crowd. Spotting who gets nervous when the Devaronian is mentioned.

control.failure: Too much noise. The encampment is a wall of anxiety and desperation — everyone looks nervous.
control.mastery: You read a micro-expression that tells you exactly who to talk to next. The trail shortens.

effect.fleeting: One person flinched when you mentioned the Devaronian. Worth following up.
effect.masterful: You identify the person who knows the most — their body language screams 'I know and I don't want to say.'
effect.legendary: You map the information flow. You know who knows what, who's lying, and who's afraid. The optimal path to Raden's location is clear.

disciplineChallenges.context: Leaning on a local who clearly knows something but won't talk.

control.failure: They shut down completely. Mouth closed, eyes down. And three people nearby saw you threaten someone — word will spread.
control.mastery: They crack immediately and talk fast. The information is detailed and terrified-accurate.

effect.fleeting: A stammered half-answer. Enough to confirm a direction but the details are garbled by fear.
effect.masterful: They talk. Fast. Clear. They want you gone and they'll say whatever makes that happen. Solid intel.
effect.legendary: They break completely. Everything they know pours out — sightings, timing, the filtration plant, and a warning: 'There are others looking for him too. Trandoshans.'

disciplineChallenges.context: Working the underworld channels. Trading favors for information. Speaking the right language to the right people.

control.failure: You're not plugged in here. Blackwind Point has its own networks and you're an outsider. No traction.
control.mastery: You find someone who owes Varth a favor. They give you everything they have on Raden's movements.

effect.fleeting: A rumor from the right circles. Raden's name is known but his location isn't common knowledge.
effect.masterful: Someone in the network tracked Raden's purchases and movements. Clear breadcrumb trail pointing toward the swamp.
effect.legendary: Full network picture. Raden's history in Blackwind Point, his falling out with Varga, where he's been hiding, and the fact that Varga already sent a kill team.

scenes.title: The Filtration Plant
scenes.subtitle: Finding Raden
scenes.readAloud:
The swamp thickens as you push past the encampment's edge. Gnarled trees hang with curtains of moss. The ground turns soft, then wet. Insects swarm in clouds around every step.

After twenty minutes of trudging through knee-deep muck, the trees open onto a clearing dominated by a rusted industrial structure — the old water filtration plant. Pipes jut from the walls at odd angles, crusted with mineral deposits. Most of the windows are dark. But through a crack in one of the maintenance shed shutters on the west side, you catch the faint glow of a charge lamp.

Something moves behind the shutter. A shadow. Then silence.

Then a voice — sharp, shaking, and speaking Basic with a Devaronian accent:

"Don't come any closer. I will shoot."
scenes.gmNotes:
ARRIVAL: If the crew reached 10 VP in the group challenge, they know exactly where Raden is hiding — the maintenance shed on the west side. They can approach directly and with confidence. If they left the encampment with fewer than 10 VP, they need to search the plant grounds. Add complications: they trip Raden's crude perimeter alarms (toppled cans on wire), hear him before they see him, or have to search multiple buildings before finding the right one.

RADEN'S STATE: Warrick Raden is a Devaronian in his forties. He's been hiding here for three days. He's sleep-deprived, malnourished, and terrified. He has a blaster aimed at the door — his hands are shaking. He set crude trip-wires around the maintenance shed and has been surviving on the ration packs he bought from Mekka.

Raden thought Varth was dead — captured by the Empire and executed. When the crew mentions Varth's name, Raden's reaction is visceral: shock, then relief, then fear again. Varth being alive means there's a plan. A plan means danger.

RADEN'S BACKSTORY: Raden was a middleman in Varga's financial network. After Varth was captured, Raden skimmed credits — thought no one was watching. Varga found out. Raden has been running ever since. He has hand-drawn maps of Varga's fortress from when he worked the operation, guard shift patterns he memorized, and intimate knowledge of Varga's paranoid habits.

THE NEGOTIATION: How the crew handles Raden determines his cooperation level:

- CALM / NEGOTIATE: Raden cooperates willingly. He provides detailed fortress intel — maps, guard rotations, Varga's daily patterns, the location of TC-663, and where guests are quartered. He's grateful and genuinely wants to help.

- THREATEN: Raden cooperates but he's resentful. He gives the minimum information needed. He looks for opportunities to undermine the crew or hedge his bets. If things go sideways inside the fortress, threatened-Raden may try to cut a deal with Varga's people.

RADEN'S OFFER: Once calmed, Raden proposes the plan: he can get the crew inside Varga's court as 'entertainment' — gladiators, gamblers, or mercenaries. Varga is hosting a delegation of high rollers and business partners. He wants spectacle. Raden can vouch for the crew's credentials.

SCENE ENDS: When Raden's cooperation is established and the plan is agreed upon. Then the Trandoshans arrive — transition to Scene 4.

npcs.name: Warrick Raden
npcs.notes: Nervous wreck. Owes Varth a debt. The heroes' entry ticket to Varga's palace.
npcs.behaviour: Terrified, armed with a blaster, jumping at shadows. Relieved when he hears Varth is alive. Agrees to help if protected.
dialogue: "Don't come any closer. I will shoot."
"Varth? He's alive? Stars help me — he's alive."
"I know how to get you in. Varga is hosting a delegation of high rollers and business partners. He wants entertainment — gladiators, gamblers, mercenaries."
"Once we're inside... you're on your own. I have debts to settle with Varga and I plan to settle them by being very far away when things go wrong."
"I skimmed credits after Varth was taken. I thought he was dead — everyone did. Varga found the discrepancy six weeks ago. I've been running ever since."
npcs.intel: Raden was a middleman in Varga's network — skimmed credits after Varth was captured. Got caught. Has hand-drawn maps of the fortress, guard shift times, and Varga's paranoid habits.

passive.name: Fixer's Contacts
passive.description: Raden knows everyone in Varga's operation. Can identify guards, routines, and weak points.

threatBuild.notes: Raden is not a fighter. His value is information, not combat.

scenes.hazards: Swamp terrain approaching the plant. Raden's crude trip-wires and perimeter alarms if crew left the encampment with < 10 VP.

decisions.choice: Calm Raden and negotiate his help
decisions.consequence: He cooperates willingly and provides detailed fortress intel — maps, guard rotations, Varga's habits, TC-663's location.

decisions.choice: Threaten Raden into compliance
decisions.consequence: He cooperates but provides minimum intel. Looks for ways to undermine or betray the crew at the first opportunity inside the fortress.

narrativeLinks.note: Trandoshan hit squad arrives at the end of this scene. Transition to combat.
Raden's cooperation level affects his helpfulness in the Court scene.

environmentMechanics.name: Swamp Approach
environmentMechanics.effect: Difficult terrain. Knee-deep muck slows movement. Insects reduce visibility at range.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Follow established paths. Locals' directions help (high VP from Scene 2).

environmentMechanics.name: Raden's Trip-Wires
environmentMechanics.trigger: Approaching the maintenance shed (only if crew has < 10 VP)
environmentMechanics.effect: Crude alarms — toppled cans on wire. Triggers noise that alerts Raden. He fires a warning shot through the door.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: With 10 VP, the crew knows about the trip-wires from Greez Thakka's description. They can avoid them.

items.name: Fortress Maps (sketch)
items.note: Raden's hand-drawn maps of Varga's fortress. Rough but useful — guard positions, guest quarters, throne room layout.

rewards.narrative: Raden cooperates. The plan takes shape — enter as entertainment, infiltrate from within. But they're not the only ones who found him.

pacing.openingBeat: The swamp. The rusted plant. A light in the maintenance shed. A shaking voice: 'I will shoot.'
pacing.risingAction: Talking Raden down. The blaster lowers inch by inch. Varth's name is the key.
pacing.climax: Raden agrees to help. The plan forms. Then — the sound of heavy boots in the swamp outside.
pacing.resolution: Transition to Scene 4. The Trandoshans have found them.

disciplineChallenges.context: Calming Raden down. He's panicking with a blaster aimed at you.

control.mastery: Raden lowers the blaster and immediately offers actionable intel — he knows a back way into Varga's fortress through the sewer outflow. Saves a planning step.

effect.masterful: Raden lowers the blaster. Starts talking. Relieved that Varth is alive. He'll cooperate — tells you about the fortress layout and his falling out with Varga.
effect.legendary: Raden breaks down with relief. Blaster clatters to the floor. He tells you everything — Varga's security, the court dynamics, the encrypted datapad location, and what Demos is really up to.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading Raden's mental state to find the right approach. What is he most afraid of?

control.failure: He's terrified — that's all you can read. Everything else is obscured by raw fear.
control.mastery: You see through the fear to the guilt underneath. Raden doesn't just want protection — he wants absolution. He betrayed Varth's network and he knows it.

effect.fleeting: He's more scared of Varga than of you. That's useful leverage.
effect.masterful: Three things: he's terrified of Varga, he's guilty about what he did to Varth's operation, and he's desperate for someone to tell him what to do. Appeal to his guilt and he'll follow.
effect.legendary: Full read. The guilt, the fear, the self-loathing. But underneath it all — a flicker of hope. He wants to make it right. Give him that chance and he's yours completely.

disciplineChallenges.context: Building a convincing cover identity for the palace infiltration. Working with Raden to craft the story.

control.failure: Cover has gaps. Varga's people may ask uncomfortable questions. Demos will be suspicious from arrival.
control.mastery: The cover identity is so convincing that Raden's vouching sounds natural. Varga's gate guards wave you through without secondary verification.

effect.fleeting: Thin but functional cover. It'll survive casual scrutiny but won't hold under interrogation. Stay low-profile.
effect.masterful: Air-tight cover. Backgrounds, references, and specialties all check out. Standard entry.
effect.legendary: The cover identity has depth — a fabricated history that even palace records corroborate. You arrive with a reputation. Varga is interested before you walk in.

scenes.title: The Hit Squad
scenes.subtitle: Protect Raden
scenes.readAloud:
Raden freezes mid-sentence. His eyes go wide.

"They found me."

You hear it now — the rhythmic squelch of heavy boots in the swamp. Multiple sets. Coming from at least two directions. Through the grimy windows of the filtration plant, you catch movement in the tree line: massive, scaled figures moving with predatory patience.

Trandoshans.

Then a deeper sound — the whine of servomotors and the grinding of armored treads. Something large pushes through the undergrowth toward the main entrance. A heavy combat droid, its photoreceptors cutting red beams through the mist.

Raden grabs your arm. "They're here to kill me. If I die, you don't get inside that fortress. Protect me."
scenes.gmNotes:
COMBAT ENCOUNTER: This is a straight fight with a protection objective. The crew must keep Raden alive while dealing with the Trandoshan hit squad and the heavy combat droid.

TRANDOSHAN TACTICS: The hit squad breaches from multiple entry points — they've been hunting Raden and they know the building. They use scent-tracking, which means steam concealment doesn't fully work against them (reduces to partial concealment). They work in pairs, using the cramped machinery to set up crossfires.

HEAVY COMBAT DROID: Blocks the main entrance. Advances slowly, absorbing fire. Defense 8. Iron Clad — uses Defense vs Evasion attacks. Vulnerable to Ion damage. The droid is the anvil; the Trandoshans are the hammer.

ENVIRONMENTAL HAZARDS: The filtration plant is full of exploitable machinery. Steam pipes can be burst deliberately (Tech check) for area denial. Catwalks can be collapsed to block approaches. Cramped corridors between machinery limit movement to single-file — good for chokepoints, bad for being flanked.

RADEN: He's not a fighter. He cowers behind cover, fires his blaster wildly if directly threatened (more dangerous to friendlies than enemies), and panics if anyone goes down. If a PC spends an action to direct him (Tactics or Leadership), he'll follow orders and stay in cover properly.

SCENE FAILURE: If Raden dies, the crew loses their easy route into Varga's court. They'd need to find another way in — significantly harder and likely requiring Adventure-level improvisation.

AFTER THE FIGHT: The hit squad is eliminated. Raden is shaken but alive. He's now fully committed — after being hunted by Varga's killers, his only safety is with the crew. He proposes the entertainment cover: present the heroes as gladiators, gamblers, or mercenaries at Varga's court.

The scene ends with the transition: the skiff ride across Nymeve Lake. Varga's fortress materializes through curtains of mist — a moss-covered stone ruin retrofitted with gaudy tech, blaster turrets, and garish banners. The next phase begins.

scenes.title: The Court of Varga
scenes.subtitle: The Throne Room
scenes.readAloud:
The skiff glides across Nymeve Lake through hanging curtains of mist. The fortress materializes ahead — a moss-covered stone ruin retrofitted with gaudy tech, blaster turrets, and gaudy banners bearing Varga's sigil. You dock at a landing platform ringed by Gamorrean guards.

Raden leads you through damp, torch-lit corridors that smell of mold and roasting meat. You emerge into Varga's Throne Room — a sensory assault of thick smoke, frantic jizz-wailer music, and a crowd of sycophants, thieves, and scavengers.

At the far end, resting on a massive reinforced hover-sled, sits Varga the Hutt. He is bloated, scarred, and flanked by a polished silver protocol droid. Varga barks in Huttese, his deep voice shaking the room. The droid translates:

"The Mighty Varga is bored with beggars. Prove you are worth his air."
scenes.gmNotes:
Varga demands an immediate demonstration. Each hero must prove their value:
- The Muscle gets a fight against a Gamorrean in the pit.
- The Face gets a battle of wits with courtiers.
- The Tech is ordered to fix Varga's hover-sled with a blaster at their head.
- Other specialists demonstrate as appropriate.

If heroes succeed, Varga welcomes them as 'guests' (prisoners with privileges). He then turns on Raden — recognizing him. Varga bellows in Huttese. Gamorreans drag Raden to the dungeons as punishment. Raden's screams serve as a warning.

TC-663 INTRODUCTION: During the audition, the silver protocol droid (TC-663 / 'Six-Six') translates for Varga. If the heroes use Switch's code phrase from Adventure 1, TC-663's photoreceptors flash recognition. He begins covertly assisting them — providing palace maps, guard rotations, and the location of Varga's encrypted datapad. Without the code phrase, TC-663 remains cautious and provides only partial assistance.

NARRATIVE FUNCTION: This scene establishes the heroes inside the fortress, introduces the key NPCs they'll interact with during the court phase, and rips away their guide (Raden) to raise the stakes.

effect.masterful: TC-663 keeps glancing at you meaningfully — that's interest, not suspicion. Igren Demos watches Varga like a predator. There's a coup brewing.

scenes.title: Currying Favor
scenes.subtitle: The Multi-Day Court Sandbox
scenes.readAloud:
Over the next several days, you navigate the dangerous politics of Varga's court. Admiral Varth coaches you over encrypted comms, pointing out targets and reminding you to keep your eyes on the prize.

The court operates on Varga's whims. Events arrive unannounced — delegations, spectacles, celebrations, punishments. Between them, the heroes have windows to explore, make contacts, and pursue the mission. Every day brings new opportunities and new dangers.
scenes.gmNotes:
This is a MULTI-DAY SANDBOX scene. The court phase plays out over 3-5 in-game days with structured events and freeform exploration between them. The GM runs events in order, with 'between' activities available during downtime.

STRUCTURED COURT EVENTS (run in order):

1. THE GLADIATOR ARENA (Day 1): Varga announces a gladiator tournament to 'test' his new guests. The heroes can fight directly (Shockboxer/Duelist spotlight) or sponsor a fighter. The arena is a sunken pit in the fortress courtyard. Winning builds reputation and earns Varga's favor. The reigning champion is Gorrak — a scarred Trandoshan veteran. Defeating him earns significant court standing.

2. THE IRON RING DELEGATION (Day 2): A Zabrak slaver named Vel Crekk leads the Iron Ring delegation into court with chained prisoners — Twi'leks, Rodians, and one massive Wookiee warrior (Grakkus). During the negotiations, a Twi'lek slave serving Varga's drinks attempts to poison the Hutt's goblet. If the heroes notice (Insight check) and intervene — either by stopping the poison or by covering for the slave — they can earn instant favor with Varga (saving his life) or with the slave (protecting him). The slave's name is Narek. The Wookiee warrior (Grakkus) is sold to Varga as arena muscle but remains unchained — he fights willingly because the Iron Ring holds his family. Grakkus stays in the fortress after the slavers leave. He is a SLEEPER THREAT who participates in the Knife in the Dark event.

3. LADY SINDE'S IMPERIAL DELEGATION (Day 3-4, final event): Lady Sinde arrives with a 4-Stormtrooper escort. She is ostensibly an Imperial logistics officer negotiating Tibanna gas shipments for Project Leviathan. Her real identity: Victra Sinde, ISB (Imperial Security Bureau) double agent embedded in the Leviathan logistics chain. She monitors Imperial-Hutt collusion for the ISB while maintaining her logistics cover. Heroes may recognize her from Organa's intelligence network or discover her ISB identity via Investigation or Stealth (eavesdropping on her encrypted comms). Sinde despises Varga — he's a tool. The Empire is already planning to discard him once Leviathan is complete.

BETWEEN-EVENT ACTIVITIES (available during downtime, any order):

• GOME THE MONEYCHANGER: An elderly Duros moneychanger who handles Varga's financial transactions. Rambling, morally indifferent, obsessed with numbers. If engaged in conversation about finances (Charm or Investigation), he reveals that Varga has departure contingency plans — a fallback headquarters on Bespin, in Cloud City. This is the critical intel that connects Adventure 2 to Adventure 3. Gome doesn't understand the significance of what he's sharing; he just likes talking about money.

• TC-663 NIGHTTIME VISIT: Between events, TC-663 visits the heroes' quarters at night. If they've activated him with Switch's code phrase, he delivers Demos's encrypted comm traffic — intercepted messages between Demos and an Imperial contact. The messages reference 'the prisoner' (Denia) and Demos's plan to sell her to the Inquisitorius. This is the first concrete evidence that someone important is hidden in the dungeons.

• KESSRA'S AVIARY AMBUSH (CONDITIONAL — only if heroes humiliated Kessra in Scene 1): Kessra has been hired as a security contractor inside Varga's fortress. She sets a personal vendetta trap — targeting 1-2 heroes by name (the ones who humiliated her). She lures them to the fortress aviary (a domed enclosure housing Takodana predatory raptors) under a false pretense (a message from 'a contact'). Once inside, she locks the doors and releases the raptors. This is NOT Varga's doing — it's personal. Kessra wants revenge, not to blow the heroes' cover. If the heroes kill the raptors and confront Kessra, she can be intimidated into backing down permanently or killed quietly. If they escape, she tries again later.

• KNIFE IN THE DARK (Night of Day 3 or 4): A nighttime assassination attempt. The Wookiee sleeper Grakkus teams with Narek the Twi'lek (if saved from the poison attempt — he switches sides out of gratitude to whoever saved him, but his gratitude makes him manipulable by the real organizer) and a Quarren assassin named Skreev. Skreev is the organizer — an agent sent by one of Varga's rivals to eliminate the heroes (who are seen as Varga's new favorites). They strike at night in the heroes' quarters. Grakkus provides muscle. The heroes must fight in close quarters, possibly in the dark, with limited weapons (their good gear may be locked away). This is a dangerous encounter — Grakkus alone is formidable.

npcs.name: Gome
npcs.notes: Elderly palace moneychanger. Rambling, morally indifferent, obsessed with numbers. Reveals Bespin/Cloud City as Varga's fallback headquarters.
npcs.behaviour: Talks endlessly about financial details if asked. Doesn't see the moral dimension of anything. Shares critical Bespin intel without realizing its importance.
dialogue: "The accounts for the secondary operations? Oh, those route through a Bespin address. Cloud City, actually. Very elegant banking infrastructure up there — did you know they use a triple-ledger system?"
"Varga keeps a contingency vault on Cloud City. Standard Hutt practice. If things go wrong here, he takes the Chariot and... well, you understand."
"I don't make moral judgments about money. Money is just numbers. Beautiful, precise numbers."

passive.name: Loves Numbers
passive.description: Gome will talk endlessly about financial details if asked. He doesn't care about crime — only about elegant accounting.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant. The only NPC who will freely discuss Varga's operations — he just doesn't see the moral dimension. CRITICAL: provides Bespin/Cloud City intel for Adv3.

decisions.consequence: Discovering her dual role provides leverage but risks drawing ISB attention to the heroes.

pacing.openingBeat: Days in Varga's court. Three structured events unfold.
pacing.risingAction: Iron Ring slavers. Lady Sinde's delegation. Gome's Bespin intel. TC-663's nighttime delivery.
pacing.climax: Knife in the Dark — assassination attempt at night. Then the dungeon investigation begins.
pacing.resolution: The heroes have intel, reputation, and a lead on the dungeons. Time to move.

disciplineChallenges.context: Fighting Gorrak in the gladiator arena.

control.failure: Gorrak wins. The hero survives (arena fights are to submission) but reputation suffers. The court sees weakness.
control.mastery: Gorrak is defeated so decisively that Varga declares a holiday. The reigning champion dethroned — every fighter in the court now respects or fears the hero.

effect.fleeting: A close, grinding fight. You win by submission in the late rounds. The crowd is satisfied but not electrified. Gorrak nods grudging respect.
effect.masterful: Decisive victory. The crowd roars. Varga is delighted. Significant court reputation earned. Gorrak offers a terse warrior's salute.
effect.legendary: You dismantle Gorrak. The Trandoshan champion — unbeaten for two years — goes down clean. The arena erupts. Varga awards you a champion's token — a gold medallion that grants passage anywhere in the fortress.

disciplineChallenges.context: Noticing Narek's poison attempt during the Iron Ring delegation.

control.failure: Nothing seems unusual. The drink reaches Varga. If poisoned, Varga collapses — chaos erupts and the heroes are prime suspects.
control.mastery: You spot the poison AND read Narek's face — this isn't malice, it's desperation. Someone put him up to this. He's terrified.

disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll only if a PC is actively watching the servants. Do not prompt.

effect.fleeting: You notice Narek's hand trembling as he serves the goblet. Something is wrong — but you can't tell what. Enough to justify intervening.
effect.masterful: You spot Narek's hand trembling as he serves the goblet. A thin film on the liquid's surface — poison. You can act before Varga drinks.
effect.legendary: You see the entire sequence: Narek palms something from his sleeve, the slight discoloration in the drink, and the way he positions himself near the exit. You can stop the poison AND cover for Narek simultaneously.

disciplineChallenges.context: Maintaining cover throughout the multi-day court phase.

control.failure: A suspicious courtier begins asking pointed questions. Demos takes notice. Cover is thinning — one more slip and you're exposed.
control.mastery: Cover holds so well that courtiers begin sharing palace gossip with you. You learn about secret passages, guard rotation gaps, and Varga's sleeping schedule.

effect.fleeting: Cover holds through casual interactions. You avoid the hard questions but you're not building trust either. Neutral standing.
effect.masterful: Cover holds perfectly throughout the stay. No suspicion raised. You blend into the court like you belong.
effect.legendary: You become a fixture. Courtiers compete to be seen with you. The cover identity takes on a life of its own — useful contacts that may outlast the mission.

disciplineChallenges.context: Discovering Lady Sinde's ISB identity through her encrypted comms or behaviour patterns.

control.failure: She appears to be exactly what she claims — a logistics officer. Clean cover.
control.mastery: You identify Sinde as ISB AND notice she's running a separate investigation — she's monitoring Varga's Leviathan connection independently. Potential ally or threat depending on approach.

effect.fleeting: Something doesn't add up — her boots are military issue, not logistics corps. She's more than she claims, but you can't prove it.
effect.masterful: Her comm frequencies don't match standard logistics channels. ISB encryption protocols. She's a double agent embedded in the Leviathan supply chain.
effect.legendary: Full intelligence profile assembled: ISB operative Victra Sinde, monitoring Imperial-Hutt collusion. Her mission parallels yours in ways that could be leveraged.

disciplineChallenges.context: Eavesdropping on Lady Sinde's private meeting with Varga.

control.failure: Spotted by a guard. Must talk your way out — Deception check or cover blown.
control.mastery: Full conversation overheard AND you observe Sinde passing a data chip to Varga under the table. Physical evidence of the deal.

effect.fleeting: Partial audio through the door. You catch the words 'next shipment' and 'the Accountant' but miss the details.
effect.masterful: Full conversation: next Tibanna shipment coordinates, payment terms, mention of 'the Accountant's records.' Solid intel.
effect.legendary: Crystal clear. You hear everything — shipment coordinates, payment routing through Bespin, Sinde's veiled threat about Imperial audits, and Varga's nervous response. You also catch a data frequency you can track later.

disciplineChallenges.context: Engaging Gome in conversation about Varga's finances.

control.failure: Gome talks about exchange rates for 20 minutes. No actionable intel. He's boring, not guarded.
control.mastery: Gome accidentally reveals the specific banking institution on Bespin: Figg & Associates. He also mentions 'the Cloud City account' has a biometric lock — Varga's voiceprint.

effect.fleeting: Gome mentions 'contingency accounts' in passing before changing the subject. Enough to confirm Varga has money elsewhere.
effect.masterful: Gome rambles about contingency accounts, departure plans, Cloud City banking. Critical Bespin intel acquired — Varga's escape route is financial.
effect.legendary: Gome gives you the full financial picture: Cloud City accounts, Bespin routing codes, the specific cantina where Varga's backup identity documents are cached, and the name of his Bespin contact.

disciplineChallenges.context: Activating TC-663 using Switch's code phrase.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Key check. The code phrase from Adv1 reduces this to T0/P0.

control.failure: TC-663 is cautious. Provides partial information only. He doesn't trust you enough for full commitment.
control.mastery: TC-663 goes all in. He provides everything he has: palace maps, guard rotations, Demos's encrypted comms, AND the location of Varga's encrypted datapad — it's in the throne room, locked in the arm of the hover-sled.

effect.fleeting: TC-663's photoreceptors flash recognition. He nods subtly and provides the location of the restricted dungeon entrance. Minimal risk to himself.
effect.masterful: TC-663's photoreceptors flash recognition. He provides Demos's encrypted comms referencing 'the prisoner' and basic guard rotation data.
effect.legendary: Full asset activation. TC-663 provides palace maps, guard rotations, Demos's comms, datapad location, AND offers to create a diversion when the heroes need to move on the dungeon.

disciplineChallenges.context: Slicing Varga's datapad to extract the encryption keys during a window of opportunity.

control.failure: Partial extraction. Silent alarm triggers. Lockdown in 5 rounds — the heroes must move NOW.
control.mastery: Clean extraction AND you discover a secondary encrypted partition — Varga's private ledger showing payments from an Imperial source codenamed 'The Accountant.' Direct evidence linking Varth.

effect.fleeting: Partial encryption key extracted before the window closes. Enough to decode some of Varth's financial records, but not all.
effect.masterful: Encryption keys extracted cleanly. No trace left. Full access to Varth's financial pipeline through Varga's network.
effect.legendary: Complete extraction — encryption keys, routing tables, and a backup of Varga's entire financial database. Evidence that could bring down the Besadii cartel's banking infrastructure.

scenes.title: Discovering Denia
scenes.subtitle: The Dungeon
scenes.readAloud:
TC-663's intercepted comms mentioned 'the prisoner' — someone hidden in the dungeon that Demos wants to sell to the Inquisitorius. You descend into the restricted lower levels, past sealed blast doors and silent guard droids.

The detention block is cold and humming with machinery. In the deepest cell, a stasis pod is hooked to life-support systems. Through the frost-covered viewport, you see a human woman in tattered Jedi robes, suspended in a Force-induced coma.

Her eyes are closed. But you feel something — a whisper at the edge of your consciousness. Not words. A feeling. Like drowning in slow motion.

In the adjacent cell, Warrick Raden huddles in the corner. Beaten, bruised, but alive. He looks up with hollow eyes.

Admiral Varth's voice comes over the encrypted comlink. The same voice as always. Calm. Efficient.

"Leave her. She's a liability. We don't have time for dead weight."

A pause. You can hear him thinking.

"Your call. But she slows us down and we're on a clock."
scenes.gmNotes:
This is the campaign's first major pivot. Two discoveries in one scene:

1. JEDI MASTER DENIA: Found in stasis since shortly after Order 66. Kept by Varga as a trophy to auction to the Inquisitorius. She's weak, barely alive, and telepathically reaching out. Varth advises abandonment: 'Leave her. She's a liability. We don't have time for dead weight.' The heroes must decide whether to rescue Denia against Varth's advice. This is the campaign's first major branch point — not a continuity-locked rescue. If rescued, she becomes a mentor and Varth's unwitting instrument. If abandoned, Varth secretly tips off Draco after the escape; Draco recovers her from the dungeon off-camera. The heroes never learn she survived until the Adv10 showdown. Either way, Varth gets what he needs.

2. WARRICK RADEN: Found beaten in the adjacent cell. The heroes rescued him from the hit squad, he got them inside, and Varga threw him in the dungeon. He's alive and can walk, but he's broken. The heroes will rescue him here — and Varth will execute him at the hangar in Scene 6.

EAVESDROP ON DEMOS/DRACO HOLO-CALL: While in the dungeon area, the heroes can overhear (or TC-663 alerts them to) a holo-call between Igren Demos and Inquisitor Draco. Demos is reporting that the prisoner is still viable. Draco's response: 'I am on my way, with the Assiduous.' This starts the DEATH CLOCK — Draco's Star Destroyer is inbound. The heroes must escape before it arrives.

VARTH'S RED FLAG #1: His voice doesn't change. That's what makes it chilling. He sounds the same as always — pragmatic, efficient, reasonable. 'Leave her. She's a liability.' He's not being cruel. He's being operational. And that's worse. If the heroes push back — 'She's a person, not dead weight' — Varth shrugs: 'Your call. But she slows us down.' He won't fight them on it. He doesn't need to. If they take her, he adapts. If they leave her, he gets what he wants through Draco. Either outcome serves him. Players should feel uncomfortable either way — whether they followed his advice or defied it.

decisions.choice: Rescue Denia and Raden against Varth's advice
decisions.consequence: Triggers silent alarm. Demos senses the Force disturbance. But gains a Jedi ally and saves a man from the dungeon. Varth adapts his plan around Denia's presence.

The heroes never learn she survived until the Adv10 showdown. The Church of the Force fills the lore-interpreter role in later adventures.

decisions.consequence: Reveals Draco is inbound with the Assiduous. Death clock starts. Critical intelligence.

narrativeLinks.note: Rescuing Denia triggers the fortress alarm and Demos's interception in Scene 5.

encounters.name: Elite Guard Droids
encounters.trigger: If alarm triggers or droids detect intruders

encounters.tactics: Droids fight methodically. Ion weapons are extremely effective.

environmentMechanics.name: Stasis Machinery
environmentMechanics.effect: Disconnecting improperly kills Denia. Tech check required.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Careful technical work. Slicer Spike provides +1 tier on the check.

environmentMechanics.name: Force Echo
environmentMechanics.effect: Force-sensitive characters feel nausea and dread. Igren Demos senses the disturbance from anywhere in the palace.

rewards.narrative: Jedi Master Denia rescued. Warrick Raden freed from the dungeons. Varth's first red flag revealed. Draco inbound.

pacing.openingBeat: The restricted lower levels. Cold, dark, humming machinery.
pacing.risingAction: Discovery of the stasis pod. Denia's telepathic plea. Raden in the adjacent cell.
pacing.climax: Varth's voice: 'Leave her. She's a liability.' The heroes decide. Eavesdrop on Demos/Draco: 'I am on my way.' The death clock starts.
pacing.resolution: The heroes leave with Denia (if rescued) or without her (if they followed Varth's advice). Raden is with them either way. The fortress is going hostile. Run.

disciplineChallenges.context: Slipping into the restricted lower levels.

control.failure: A guard sees movement. Must be dealt with quietly — alert the block and the whole plan goes sideways.
control.mastery: You identify and avoid every guard patrol pattern AND discover an unmonitored ventilation shaft that provides a secondary exit route.

effect.fleeting: You slip past the first checkpoint but nearly alert a droid patrol. Tight margins. One more mistake and you're made.
effect.masterful: Clean entry. No guards notice. You reach the detention block undetected.
effect.legendary: Ghost movement. You map the entire guard patrol pattern as you move through it. When you need to leave, you'll know exactly when every corridor is clear.

disciplineChallenges.context: Disabling the stasis pod's life support to free Denia safely.

control.failure: Alarm triggers. Denia's vitals spike dangerously. Igren Demos senses the Force disturbance. Lockdown imminent — move NOW.
control.mastery: Perfect disconnect. You also extract the pod's medical logs — 2 years of data showing Demos was conducting crude Force experiments on her. Evidence of his agenda.

effect.fleeting: Life support disconnects safely but the pod's alarm chirps once before you silence it. Denia stirs. The clock just got shorter.
effect.masterful: Clean disconnect. Denia wakes slowly, disoriented but alive. No alarms triggered. The pod powers down silently.
effect.legendary: Textbook medical extraction. Denia wakes coherent — still weak, but she can walk unassisted and her first words are useful: 'The Neimoidian... he's been in my dreams. He's coming.'

disciplineChallenges.context: Bypassing the high-security cell locks.

control.failure: Lock triggers backup alarm. Guards dispatched. 3 rounds before they arrive.
control.mastery: Locks open silently AND you disable the cell block's internal alarm system entirely. No alerts from this section regardless of what happens next.

effect.fleeting: One lock opens but the second jams halfway. You can force it — noisy — or try again. The clock is ticking.
effect.masterful: Locks open silently. No alarm. Both cells accessible.
effect.legendary: You bypass the locks so cleanly that you can re-lock them behind you — making it look like no one was ever here. Buys extra time before the escape is discovered.

disciplineChallenges.context: Stabilizing Denia after ~2 years in stasis.

control.failure: Denia is barely conscious. Must be carried. All movement checks during the escape are stepped up in difficulty.
control.mastery: Denia is stabilized AND you administer a stim that clears her confusion. She provides telepathic tactical awareness during the escape — the party can't be ambushed.

effect.fleeting: Denia is conscious but disoriented. She can stand with support but can't move fast. She's dead weight in a sprint.
effect.masterful: Denia is weak but mobile. Can walk with support. Her eyes are focused — she knows where she is and what's happening.
effect.legendary: Denia recovers faster than expected. She walks under her own power and immediately begins using passive Force senses — she can feel the guards' positions in the corridors ahead.

disciplineChallenges.context: Resisting Denia's telepathic plea — it's emotionally overwhelming.

control.failure: The emotional weight is staggering. [Rattled] until you act on it or leave the area.
control.mastery: You not only resist but form a brief telepathic bond with Denia. She shares a flash-image: a crescent-shaped shadow against stars. You don't know what it means yet.

effect.fleeting: The wave of grief and terror hits hard. You stagger but keep your feet. Decision-making is clouded for a moment but you push through.
effect.masterful: You feel her pain but maintain composure. Clear decision-making. The empathic contact is intense but manageable.
effect.legendary: You absorb the pain and ground it. Denia senses your stability and reaches toward it — her telepathic plea shifts from desperation to trust. She stops broadcasting to the entire block.

disciplineChallenges.context: Eavesdropping on Demos's holo-call with Inquisitor Draco.

control.failure: Partial audio. Know someone Imperial is coming but not the timeline. The urgency is vague.
control.mastery: Full conversation overheard AND you capture the transmission frequency. Can be tracked later to locate Draco's fleet movements.

effect.fleeting: Muffled audio through the wall. You catch 'Inquisitorius' and 'en route.' Enough to know something bad is coming.
effect.masterful: Full conversation overheard. Draco: 'I am on my way, with the Assiduous.' Death clock confirmed — hours, not days.
effect.legendary: Crystal clear audio. Full conversation including Draco's estimated arrival window, the Assiduous's current position, and Demos's offer to 'deliver the Jedi personally.' You know exactly how long you have.

scenes.title: The Escape
scenes.subtitle: The Majordomo's Stand
scenes.readAloud:
Klaxons scream through the stone corridors. The fortress goes into lockdown — blast doors grinding shut, red emergency lighting flooding every hall.

Blocking your path stands Igren Demos, the Neimoidian Majordomo. He is no longer simpering. He holds a vibro-staff with lethal confidence, flanked by his elite Trandoshan bodyguards.

His red eyes widen as he senses the Jedi behind you. "The Jedi... she's awake. Give her to me. I will take her to the Inquisitorius myself and claim what Varga never could."
scenes.gmNotes:
Demos is the mini-boss of Adventure 2. He reveals his Force-sensitivity (untrained) and uses minor Force powers — Force Slam to separate heroes, Force Pull to yank objects or people. His elite Trandoshan bodyguards provide additional threat.

DEMOS'S MOTIVATION: He wants Denia for himself. He plans to turn her over to the Inquisitorius and buy his way into Imperial favor. He's deluding himself — the Empire is deeply speciesist and doesn't trust Neimoidians (associated with the Separatist cause). They would take Denia and discard Demos. He dies chasing a reward he'd never receive — which quietly mirrors what the heroes are doing by chasing Varth's payday.

PERSUASION OPTION: A Tier 2 Persuasion check can convince Demos to stand down by pointing out that Varga has already fled without him — Demos has been abandoned. If successful, Demos hesitates, realizes the truth, and steps aside in bitter rage. If failed, he fights to the death.

DENIA'S MOMENT (if rescued): When Demos falls (or stands down), Denia senses his untrained Force sensitivity. 'He was touched by the Force... untrained, but there.' This humanizes the kill and establishes Denia's sensitivity. IF DENIA WAS ABANDONED: Demos still fights to block the escape (he doesn't know the heroes left Denia). His death plays the same, but without Denia's moment of empathy. The kill is just a kill — no one to sense what he was.

Running battle through sealed corridors. Gamorreans and Trandoshans converge. Speed is more valuable than kills. Every round spent fighting is a round Varga gains toward escape.

npcs.notes: Demos's personal muscle. Same stats as Scene 1 Trandoshan Mercenaries.

maneuver.name: Intimidating Advance
maneuver.description: Modifies Move. Move 1 zone toward a target. That target must succeed on a Grit check vs Threat's Power or gain [Rattled].

gambit.name: Brutal Strike
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Staggered] Tactical.

exploit.name: Predator's Opening
exploit.trigger: A PC in the same zone takes damage from another source this round.
exploit.description: Immediately make an attack against that PC at -2 Power.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Brutes. Bodyguard behavior — they protect Demos.

scenes.hazards: Force powers — Slam and Pull. Environmental hazards (braziers, statues). Narrow corridors favor Demos's staff. Fortress lockdown — blast doors sealing, hostile forces converging.

decisions.choice: Fight Demos head-on
decisions.consequence: Tough fight but clears the path. Risk: Varga gains more escape time.

decisions.choice: Convince Demos that Varga has abandoned him
decisions.consequence: Persuasion T2 — if successful, Demos steps aside in bitter rage. Saves time and lives.

decisions.choice: Split the party — some fight, some run for the hangar
decisions.consequence: Risky but potentially catches Varga. Smaller groups are more vulnerable.

encounters.name: Igren Demos Showdown
encounters.trigger: Heroes encounter Demos in the corridor
encounters.description: Demos with vibro-staff and minor Force powers, flanked by 2 Trandoshan bodyguards. Mini-boss fight.
encounters.tactics: Demos uses Force Slam to separate the party. Bodyguards flank isolated targets. Focus fire on the strongest-looking PC.

environmentMechanics.name: Narrow Corridor
environmentMechanics.effect: Only 2 characters can engage Demos simultaneously. Limits flanking.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use ranged attacks from behind the front line.

environmentMechanics.name: Force Hazards
environmentMechanics.effect: Braziers and statues become projectiles. Environmental damage in the zone.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Take cover behind pillars. Force projectiles are indirect.

environmentMechanics.name: Blast Door Lockdown
environmentMechanics.effect: Sealed durasteel doors block major corridors. Athletics or Tech to bypass.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Fusion Cutter cuts through in 1 action. Tech hack opens them remotely.

rewards.narrative: Demos is defeated or persuaded to stand down. Path to the hangar is clear. If Denia is with the party, she senses his Force sensitivity — a quiet, humanizing moment.

pacing.openingBeat: Klaxons. Red light. Demos blocks the corridor. He is no longer simpering.
pacing.risingAction: Force powers and bodyguards. A real fight. Or a desperate argument.
pacing.climax: Demos is cornered — fight or talk?
pacing.resolution: Demos falls or surrenders. Denia's quiet observation. The hangar awaits.

disciplineChallenges.context: Resisting Demos's Force Slam.

control.failure: Knocked back 1 zone. [Disoriented] for 1 round. Demos presses the advantage.
control.mastery: You don't just resist — you advance through it. The Force push breaks around you. Demos's eyes go wide. He realizes he's outmatched.

effect.fleeting: You skid backward but stay on your feet. The Force push stings but doesn't break your stance. Still in the fight.
effect.masterful: Stand your ground. The Force push washes over you like a strong wind. Nothing more.
effect.legendary: You walk straight through it. The Force slam hits you and you don't even slow down. Every witness — Demos, his bodyguards, your allies — sees it. The balance of the fight shifts.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading Demos's Force abilities — is he trained?

control.failure: Terrifying display. His abilities seem formidable. No way to tell if he's trained or not.
control.mastery: Untrained AND desperate. You read his deeper motivation: he doesn't believe the Inquisitorius will accept him. He's deluding himself. This knowledge fuels a devastating Persuasion attempt.

effect.fleeting: His technique is sloppy — there's no Form behind it. He's dangerous but not precise. That's something.
effect.masterful: Untrained. Powerful raw talent but no discipline. His attacks are predictable — he telegraphs every Force push with his whole body.
effect.legendary: Completely untrained, completely delusional. He thinks the Inquisitorius will welcome him. You see a scared man playing at being powerful. Every vulnerability is visible.

disciplineChallenges.context: Convincing Demos to stand down — Varga has already abandoned him.

control.failure: Demos is too far gone. 'You don't understand what I'm owed!' Fights to the death.
control.mastery: Demos not only stands down but provides intel: the passage to the hangar is clear on the left corridor. He warns you about Sinde's Stormtroopers. He's done.

effect.fleeting: Demos hesitates. The vibro-staff lowers an inch. He's listening — but he needs more. Follow-up argument needed.
effect.masterful: Demos hesitates. Realizes Varga is fleeing without him. Steps aside in bitter rage. 'Go. Take the Jedi. It doesn't matter anymore.'
effect.legendary: Demos crumbles. The truth hits him: Varga never valued him, the Empire never will, and the Inquisitorius would discard him. He steps aside and tells you everything he knows about the hangar defenses.

disciplineChallenges.context: Forcing through blast doors during fortress lockdown.

control.failure: Door holds. Must find alternate route — costs a round. Every round is a round Varga gains.
control.mastery: Door forced open so hard it jams in the open position. Can't be re-sealed behind you — but it also can't be sealed against your retreat.

effect.fleeting: You wedge the door open enough to squeeze through one at a time. Slow but functional.
effect.masterful: Door forced open. Path cleared. The group moves through at full speed.
effect.legendary: You rip the door mechanism off its track. It crashes open and stays open. The corridor ahead is completely clear.

disciplineChallenges.context: Hacking lockdown controls to open sealed corridors.

control.failure: Partial override. Some doors open, others stay sealed. Zigzag route to the hangar.
control.mastery: Full override AND you lock the doors behind you — sealing pursuers out. The corridor becomes a one-way passage to the hangar.

effect.fleeting: One door opens. The next corridor's blast door is still sealed. Incremental progress.
effect.masterful: Blast doors retract. Clear path to the hangar wing. Standard lockdown protocols disabled for this corridor.
effect.legendary: Full fortress lockdown override from this terminal. Every blast door on the hangar level opens simultaneously. You also trigger a fire suppression system in the barracks level — chaos covers your escape.

scenes.title: The Hangar Bay
scenes.subtitle: Climax
scenes.readAloud:
You burst through the blast doors into the humid, open-air hangar. You're too late — Varga's massive luxury yacht, the Glorious Chariot, is already hovering on repulsors, engines flaring blue-white. The boarding ramp retracts as the gold-and-purple hull begins to rise.

But it's not just Varga. Lady Sinde has ordered her Stormtrooper escort to secure the hangar and eliminate all witnesses.

Blaster fire erupts from behind crates of Tibanna gas as white-armored figures take firing positions.

Warrick Raden stumbles onto the ship's ramp behind you, blinking in the harsh light. For the first time since the dungeon, he looks hopeful.

Then Admiral Varth steps forward. He looks at Raden. Almost warmly.

"Hello, Warrick." A pause. "I told you the next time I saw you, I would kill you."

The blaster shot echoes across the hangar. Raden drops. Varth doesn't break stride.
scenes.gmNotes:
This is the climax of Adventure 2 — three things happen simultaneously:

1. VARGA ESCAPES: The Glorious Chariot lifts off. Varga is fleeing to his fallback on Bespin (Cloud City). The heroes cannot stop him here — his escape is narratively required. He collapses part of the ceiling with the yacht's exhaust to discourage pursuit.

2. SINDE'S STORMTROOPERS: Lady Sinde has ordered her 4-Stormtrooper escort to eliminate all witnesses. She is protecting Project Leviathan's secrecy. The heroes must fight across the hangar using crates and equipment for cover. Tibanna gas crates are explosive if hit by blaster fire — area denial that works both ways.

3. VARTH EXECUTES RADEN: This is the second red flag — and far worse than the first. The heroes just rescued Raden from the dungeons. They've been protecting him since Scene 1. He's walking toward freedom on the ship's ramp. Varth greets him, then draws and shoots him between the eyes. 'I told you the next time I saw you, I would kill you.' The line references their last dealing — when Varth discovered Raden was running his own skim. It sounds personal. It's not. It's operational. Varth is eliminating a liability who knew too much about the embezzlement pipeline. Varth does not explain or apologize. He checks the body once and moves to the ship.

TRAJECTORY LOCK: If the heroes get to their ship fast enough, a Tactics check gets a partial trajectory lock on Varga's escape vector. The vector aligns with the Anoat Sector. Bespin. Cloud City. This connects to Gome's intel from Scene 3.

ENDING: The adventure ends on the ship, in hyperspace. Raden is dead. Varth is analyzing the trajectory data. The heroes have partial encryption keys (or not), a trajectory to Bespin, and two disturbing memories of the man they're working for.

npcs.name: Lady Sinde
npcs.notes: Ordering the elimination of all witnesses. (Recurring — same stat block as Scene 3.)

npcEffects.masterful: Chassis damage + one ally within 2 zones gains [Optimized] on next action
npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + all allies within 2 zones gain [Optimized] on next action
